short: Splitting long documents into shorter segments that can be embedded and retrieved.
definition: Document chunking divides long texts into shorter segments that fit within a model's context window and can be individually embedded and indexed for retrieval. Strategies range from fixed-size splitting to sentence- or paragraph-based splitting to semantic chunking, which groups topically coherent passages. Chunk size and overlap are key settings, since they shape retrieval precision and recall in retrieval-augmented generation systems.
